key_insight: Partial-structure R1 (pR1) can guide the assembly of small-molecule crystal structures by orienting or placing fragments, especially when heavy atoms are present.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical crystallographic strategy for solving difficult structures by incrementally building a partial model rather than relying on full direct solution.
method: Start assembly with large fragments or fragments containing heavy atoms.
Attach additional fragments to a partial model by optimizing only their orientation when possible.
For light-atom-only fragments in heavy-atom structures, use residual reflection intensities after subtracting the partial model contribution.
evidence_signal: The paper uses a few concrete examples to demonstrate that these pR1-based assembly steps are effective for solving small-molecule crystal structures.
watch_item: Light-atom fragment placement requires residual intensities after heavy-atom contributions are removed, so the approach depends on having a sufficiently accurate partial model.
paper_title: A Showcase of Using the Partial-Structure R1 to Assemble Small-Molecule Crystal Structures
